title: Meteor vs Meteorite Explained
script_summary: Clarifies the difference between meteors and meteorites while engaging the audience's curiosity.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about meteors and meteorites than social media does.
A meteor is a space rock entering Earth's atmosphere, creating a bright streak of light.
But once it lands, it’s called a meteorite. That’s the key difference.
Contrary to popular belief, not every shooting star is a meteorite.
Meteors burn up in the atmosphere, while meteorites can tell us about the solar system.
Want to learn more about space? Hit that follow button!

full_voiceover: Science has a clearer story about meteors and meteorites than social media does. A meteor is a space rock entering Earth's atmosphere, creating a bright streak of light. But once it lands, it’s called a meteorite. That’s the key difference. Contrary to popular belief, not every shooting star is a meteorite. Meteors burn up in the atmosphere, while meteorites can tell us about the solar system. Want to learn more about space? Hit that follow button!
